title: The Ball
notes: Played through intro and started playing game but crashed to desktop after about 15 minutes. Haven't tested further.

title: The Ball
notes: Game launched and ran fine. I played about 2 hours so far without any problems.
On first launch an error message about .NET framework appears, but this can be ignored and doesn't pop up again. The game never ran well on windows and it doesn't run much worse on Steam Play.
title: The Ball

notes: Error about .NET on first run. If using "Disable D3d11", a blank window will show up and nothing will happen. Otherwise, it won't even get that far without crashing.
title: The Ball

notes: Didn't used to run, but started working fine for me AFTER I upgraded my kernel from 4.14.77 to 4.19.4 and my GPU driver from 3.0 Mesa 18.2.3 to 3.0 Mesa 18.2.5. The Disable D3d11 setting is required for it to run. Performance isn't great, but it is playable with settings and resolution turned all the way down. I don't know if that's because of Proton or my hardware, and I don't typically run Windows on this machine and can't compare.
title: The Ball

notes: Everything works fine from Campaign to Survival. Game may crash sometimes in Survival but it is a game engine bug and not a Wine bug.
